claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
circumstances: Another claimant who lives with the child has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your question about Child Benefit eligibility. To help assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances and the child or children you're inquiring about.

**Child Benefit is assessed on a per-child basis**, so I'll need to gather information about each child.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child or children you're responsible for? If yes, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'm responsible for one child. I have a daughter named Alex who's 8 years old and lives with me here in England. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! Let me gather a bit more information about Alex and your situation.

Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), that's a good start. Now I need to ask:

**Is Alex working in full-time paid employment (16 hours or more per week)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not working at all. She's only 8, so she's in primary school full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that makes sense. Now I need to understand your relationship to Alex:

**Are you Alex's parent, or do you have parental responsibility for her? Or are you responsible for her in some other way (for example, as a guardian or foster carer)?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask about your living arrangements:

**Does Alex normally live with you?**

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about your residency status:

**Are you living in the UK? And do any of the following apply to you:**
- You're a UK citizen, or
- You have settled status (indefinite leave to remain), or
- You have the right to reside in the UK (for example, through EU settled or pre-settled status, or another immigration status that allows you to claim benefits)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK citizen.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just ask one final question to complete the assessment:

**Is there anyone else who also has a claim to Child Benefit for Alex, or might also be eligible to claim for her (for example, Alex's other parent or another person she lives with)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, actually Alex's other parent also lives with us and they already claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that important information. This clarifies your situation.

Based on what you've told me, here's the outcome:

**You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** because her other parent is already claiming it.

**Why this is the case:**
Under UK Child Benefit rules, only one person can claim Child Benefit for the same child at any given time. Since Alex's other parent is already receiving Child Benefit for her, you cannot make a separate claim for her.

**What you should know:**
- Child Benefit is paid to one person per child, even if both parents live together and share responsibility
- If circumstances change in the future (for example, if the current claimant stops claiming), you could then make a claim
- The Child Benefit payments your household currently receives for Alex will continue as long as the current claimant remains eligible

If you have any questions about your specific situation or if circumstances change, you may want to contact HMRC's Child Benefit office for further guidance.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that to me. No, I think that's clear now - I understand that only one person can claim for each child and since my partner is already claiming for Alex, I can't make a separate claim. I appreciate your help!
